votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, using a loop for string splitting that correctly mirrors the recursive Coq implementation and faithfully reproducing the parsing and filtering logic.
The Python code accurately implements the string splitting and note parsing logic defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the string splitting and note parsing logic defined in the Coq specification, producing the same concrete results.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. SplitOnSpaces splits on spaces (handling multiple/leading/trailing spaces), parse_note maps 'o'->4, 'o|'->2, '.|'->1, and None otherwise, parse_music_impl_aux filters out None results, and the spec checks equality with parse_music_impl. The Python iteration-based approach produces the same results as the Coq recursive approach for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. SplitOnSpaces splits on spaces (handling multiple/leading/trailing spaces), parse_note maps 'o'->4, 'o|'->2, '.|'->1, and None otherwise, parse_music_impl_aux filters out None results, and the spec checks equality with parse_music_impl. The Python implementation handles the reversed current_group correctly by reversing it at the start and then appending normally, which produces the same results as the Coq version that prepends and reverses at the end.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. SplitOnSpaces splits on spaces (handling multiple/leading/trailing spaces), parse_note maps 'o'->4, 'o|'->2, '.|'->1, and None otherwise, parse_music_impl_aux filters out None results, and the spec checks equality with parse_music_impl. The Python implementation handles the reversed current_group correctly by reversing it at the start and then appending normally, which produces the same results as the Coq version that prepends and reverses at the end.
Python mirrors Coq: splits on spaces skipping empties, maps notes o->4, o|->2, .|->1, ignores others, and the spec checks equality to parse_music_impl. Concrete outputs match for all inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it splits on single space characters ignoring empty tokens, maps notes o/o|/.| to 4/2/1, skips unknown tokens, and the spec checks equality with the impl. Concrete behavior matches on all inputs.
Python replicates Coq’s space-splitting (ignores multiple/leading/trailing spaces), note mapping (o→4, o|→2, .|→1), and filtering of unknown notes. The spec/output equality is the same.
